description.value:
The passes above Skjoldfjell have weather that behaves as though it is personally irritated. A torch lasts four seconds. An ordinary lantern is next. Skjoldfjell lamplighters long ago solved this problem through the application of copper, thick glass, a very specific valve arrangement, and a sealed oil reservoir that feeds by gravity and doesn't care what the wind is doing.

This project produces a Skjoldfjellsmål storm-lantern: a copper-body lantern with thick borosilicate-equivalent glass panels, a gravity-fed oil reservoir, and a wind-locking valve system. The lantern's flame cannot be extinguished by any natural wind, including severe mountain gales. It burns standard lamp oil at a normal rate (6 hours per flask). The copper body retains heat, and in cold environments the lantern also serves as a rudimentary hand-warmer. Heroes using the lantern in harsh weather do not have their light source disrupted by environmental conditions.

Rules Note: Cannot be extinguished by wind of any natural strength. Burns oil at normal rate (6 hours/flask). In temperatures below freezing, grants +1 to Stamina tests against cold exposure when held. Immune to rain extinguishing due to sealed valve design.

system.prerequisites: Copper sheet stock, borosilicate or thick clear glass panels, fine metalworking tools (mechanics or jewelry), gravity-feed oil reservoir fitting, fine valve mechanism.
system.projectSource: Skjoldfjellsmål Lamplighter's Record — maintained by the highland lamplighter's guild, a small and earnest organization that considers itself the most important guild in the mountains (correctly).

yield.display: Skjoldfjellsmål storm-lantern: wind-proof, rain-proof, standard oil burn rate. Handwarmer in sub-freezing conditions.
